mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate is inversely correlated with S&P 500 forward 20-day returns because a higher discount rate reduces the present value of future earnings, holding across all regimes without additional conditioning.
failure_condition: Rank IC of FEDFUNDS vs. forward 20-day returns is greater than -0.02 over any rolling 2-year period, or the hit rate of negative returns when FEDFUNDS is above median is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.5 million units indicate strong residential investment and consumer demand, which leads to positive equity market returns over the subsequent month as economic momentum persists.
failure_condition: In months where HOUST exceeds 1500, the proportion of positive forward 20-day S&P 500 returns is less than 55%, or the mean return is not significantly different from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment and rising real retail sales signals an overheated economy where labor constraints push up costs, eroding profit margins and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter horizon.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average forward 60-day return is positive, or the rank IC for the interaction term (product of standardized UNRATE and RSAFS) is less than 0.05 absolute value over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During an industrial recovery (INDPRO above 105), rising payroll employment feeds into robust consumer spending, driving higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When INDPRO > 105, the correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward 20-day returns is less than 0.1 or the signal has a hit rate below 50% after controlling for overall market trend.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals robust manufacturing activity and economic strength, translating into immediate positive equity returns within a week as market participants price in continued growth.
failure_condition: The weekly rank IC of INDPRO changes vs. forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year window, or the mean forward return after positive INDPRO surprises is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5%, the labor market is sufficiently tight that wage pressures begin to compress profit margins and the Fed may lean towards tightening, both headwinds for equities over the following month.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE is below 5%, the average forward 20-day S&P 500 return is positive, or the proportion of negative returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and consumer prices indicate overheating, prompting expectations of monetary tightening that depress equity valuations over a quarter horizon.
failure_condition: When both proxies are rising, the average forward 60-day return is positive, or the interaction term (INDPRO growth * CPIAUCSL growth) has a rank IC below 0.03 absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4%, rising consumer price inflation exacerbates real rate negativity and forces the central bank to maintain a tight stance, compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4%, the correlation between CPIAUCSL month-over-month and forward 20-day returns is not negative (i.e., rank IC > 0) over any 2-year window.
